issue_title: [ML] [Inference API] The `_stream` API doesn't consistently return the elastic product header
issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

8.16.0 and onward

### Installed Plugins

_No response_

### Java Version

_bundled_

### OS Version

N/A

### Problem Description

The api inconsistently returns the `X-elastic-product` header in the response.

The API should always return the header.

### Steps to Reproduce

Create an openai endpoint
```
PUT http://localhost:9200/_inference/test
{
    "service": "openai",
    "task_type": "completion",
    "service_settings": {
        "api_key": "<api key>",
        "model_id": "gpt-4o"
    }
}
```

Make a request
```
POST http://localhost:9200/_inference/completion/test/_stream
{
    "input": "What's the best American food?"
}
```

Observe that the header isn't always returned.

### Logs (if relevant)

_No response_

commit_message:
block-writes cannot be added after read-only (#119007)

Fix bug in ReindexDataStreamIndexAction. If the source index has
both a block-writes and is read-only, these must be updated on
the destination index. If read-only is set first, the block-writes
cannot be added because settings cannot be modified.

issue_title: Server error while parsing ESQL query
issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

9.0

### Installed Plugins

_No response_

### Java Version

_bundled_

all

### Problem Description

The following error was observed. Unfortunately the query that leads to this is not known (the author was experimenting and does not remember what it was), it is possible that it is an invalid query. In that case this error should not be a server error but a client error.

commit_message:
ESQL: Catch parsing exception (#124958)

When an invalid popMode occurs (due to an invalid query), ANTLR throws
 an out-of-channel exception, bypassing the existing checks.
This PR extends the checks and properly reports the error back to the
 user

Fix #119025

issue_title: Downsampling fails for indexes configured with [index.mapping.ignore_malformed:true]
issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

8.17

### Installed Plugins

_No response_

### Java Version

_bundled_

### OS Version

all

### Problem Description

For TSDS configured with `[index.mapping.ignore_malformed:true]`, downsampling fails to create downsampled indexes as this setting is not allowed for `@timestamp` field:

```

	at org.elasticsearch.xpack.downsample.TransportDownsampleAction.lambda$masterOperation$6(TransportDownsampleAction.java:338) ~[?:?]
	at org.elasticsearch.action.ActionListenerImplementations$ResponseWrappingActionListener.onResponse(ActionListenerImplementations.java:247) ~[elasticsearch-8.16.1.jar:?]

	at org.elasticsearch.action.admin.indices.mapping.get.TransportGetMappingsAction.doMasterOperation(TransportGetMappingsAction.java:31) ~[elasticsearch-8.16.1.jar:?]
	at org.elasticsearch.action.support.master.info.TransportClusterInfoAction.masterOperation(TransportClusterInfoAction.java:65) ~[elasticsearch-8.16.1.jar:?]
	at org.elasticsearch.action.support.master.info.TransportClusterInfoAction.masterOperation(TransportClusterInfoAction.java:25) ~[elasticsearch-8.16.1.jar:?]
	at org.elasticsearch.action.support.master.TransportMasterNodeAction.executeMasterOperation(TransportMasterNodeAction.java:127) ~[elasticsearch-8.16.1.jar:?]
	at org.elasticsearch.action.support.master.TransportMasterNodeAction$AsyncSingleAction.lambda$doStart$3(TransportMasterNodeAction.java:238) ~[elasticsearch-8.16.1.jar:?]

	at java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:1575) ~[?:?]
```

This happens even if the TSDS has `@timestamp` explicitly configured with `[ignore_malformed:false]`.

### Steps to Reproduce

Create a TSDS with index setting `[index.mapping.ignore_malformed:true]` and `@timestamp` configured with `[ignore_malformed:false]`. 

Trigger a downsample action.

_No response_

issue_title: ESQL: Expand docs on LIKE and backslash escaping a little
issue_body:
The escaping of the backslash (which is the escape character itself) in LIKE can lead to confusion, esp. when users deal with Windows-style file paths like `C:\Windows\System32`. A literal backslash sent via cURL turns into a whopping 8 backslashes.

Let's add an example to our docs and mention escaping of `\` specifically.

C.f. a bug report that ran into this issue here: https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/118932
commit_message:

issue_title: ESQL: LOOKUP JOIN push down optimizations
issue_body:
Compared to `ENRICH` (and other, similar plans like `GROK`, `DISSECT` and `EVAL`), we do not push down `LOOKUP JOIN`s.

That leads to suboptimal plans, e.g. because projections before and after the `LOOKUP JOIN` cannot be combined.

Example:
```
              FROM test
            | LOOKUP JOIN lookup_index1 ON field
            | RENAME foo AS b
            | LOOKUP JOIN lookup_index2 ON field
            | DROP b*
```
The `RENAME foo AS b` in between the `LOOKUP JOIN`s becomes an `EsqlProject`, which cannot be combined with the `EsqlProject` that comes from the `DROP b*` because the `LOOKUP JOIN lookup_index_2` is in the way. As a result, we cannot determine that the field `foo` obtained from the `lookup_index1` is actually dropped and unused in the end.

**Edit:** Let's also double check that there are no other existing optimizer rules that could (somewhat) easily be applied to `LOOKUP JOIN`.
commit_message:
ESQL: Pushdown Lookup Join past Project (#129503)

Add a new logical plan optimization:

When there is a Project (KEEP/DROP/RENAME/renaming EVALs) in a LOOKUP JOIN's left child (the "main" side), perform the Project after the LOOKUP JOIN. This prevents premature field extractions when the lookup join happens on data nodes.

issue_title: Flatten graph token filter returning multi-position tokens
issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

8.17.0

### Installed Plugins

_No response_

### Java Version

_bundled_

### OS Version

Elastic Cloud

### Problem Description

While experimenting with the [Flatten graph token filter](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/8.17/analysis-flatten-graph-tokenfilter.html) example, I noticed an unexpected behavior. Despite including `flatten_graph` in the filter list, the `_analyze` response still contained multi-position tokens. Specifically, the token **dns** had a `positionLength` of 3, indicating it was still a multi-position token. A comparison of the responses with and without the filter revealed no difference in the JSON output.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Run the following `_analyze` Request:

  ```
GET /_analyze
{
  "tokenizer": "standard",
  "filter": [
    {
      "type": "synonym_graph",
      "synonyms": [ "dns, domain name system" ]
    }
  ],
  "text": "domain name system is fragile"
}
  ```
Response:

```json
{
  "tokens": [
    {
      "token": "dns",
      "start_offset": 0,
      "end_offset": 18,
      "type": "SYNONYM",
      "position": 0,
      "positionLength": 3
    },
    {
      "token": "domain",
      "start_offset": 0,
      "end_offset": 6,
      "type": "<ALPHANUM>",
      "position": 0
    },
    {
      "token": "name",
      "start_offset": 7,
      "end_offset": 11,
      "type": "<ALPHANUM>",
      "position": 1
    },
    {
      "token": "system",
      "start_offset": 12,
      "end_offset": 18,
      "type": "<ALPHANUM>",
      "position": 2
    },
    {
      "token": "is",
      "start_offset": 19,
      "end_offset": 21,
      "type": "<ALPHANUM>",
      "position": 3
    },
    {
      "token": "fragile",
      "start_offset": 22,
      "end_offset": 29,
      "type": "<ALPHANUM>",
      "position": 4
    }
  ]
}
```

2. Add the `flatten_graph` filter:

  ```
GET /_analyze
{
  "tokenizer": "standard",
  "filter": [
    {
      "type": "synonym_graph",
      "synonyms": [ "dns, domain name system" ]
    },
    "flatten_graph"
  ],
  "text": "domain name system is fragile"
}
  ```
Response:

```json
{
  "tokens": [
    {
      "token": "dns",
      "start_offset": 0,
      "end_offset": 18,
      "type": "SYNONYM",
      "position": 0,
      "positionLength": 3
    },
    {
      "token": "domain",
      "start_offset": 0,
      "end_offset": 6,
      "type": "<ALPHANUM>",
      "position": 0
    },
    {
      "token": "name",
      "start_offset": 7,
      "end_offset": 11,
      "type": "<ALPHANUM>",
      "position": 1
    },
    {
      "token": "system",
      "start_offset": 12,
      "end_offset": 18,
      "type": "<ALPHANUM>",
      "position": 2
    },
    {
      "token": "is",
      "start_offset": 19,
      "end_offset": 21,
      "type": "<ALPHANUM>",
      "position": 3
    },
    {
      "token": "fragile",
      "start_offset": 22,
      "end_offset": 29,
      "type": "<ALPHANUM>",
      "position": 4
    }
  ]
}
```

3. Examine responses generated in Steps 1 and 2

### Logs (if relevant)

_No response_

commit_message:
ESQL: Fix MultiClusterSpecIT failing test using :: cast (#119263)

Fixes https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/119254
Fixes https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/119255

The `x::<type>` cast syntax doesn't exist in older versions, so, if the test doesn't require a modern capability, it may fail.

I'm fixing this in main instead of 8.x just to avoid future conflicts, and because the cast syntax has nothing to do with the test itself.

commit_message:
ESQL: Fix MultiClusterSpecIT failing test using :: cast (#119263)

Fixes https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/119254
Fixes https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/119255

The `x::<type>` cast syntax doesn't exist in older versions, so, if the test doesn't require a modern capability, it may fail.

I'm fixing this in main instead of 8.x just to avoid future conflicts, and because the cast syntax has nothing to do with the test itself.

commit_message:
Remove synthetic role names for legacy API keys  (#119844)

This PR addresses an issue where legacy API keys fail consistency checks
because they include synthetic role names. 

We removed synthetic role names with
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/56005. We added
consistency checks sometime later to enforce no role names, with
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/93894 in `8.8.0`. 

Rather than relaxing our consistency checks, this PR tweaks
de-serialization logic to strip out role names when appropriate. This
has the advantage that we maintain the invariant the consistency check
is meant to enforce.

Note that this does not manifest in production: outside of RCS 2.0, we
only execute consistency checks with assertions enabled. For RCS 2.0, an
API key would require `remote_indices` privileges to ever be sent cross
cluster and go through consistency checks. These were introduced after
we've stopped including role names in API keys so it's not a real issue
either.

Closes: https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/119259 Closes:
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/119435 Closes:
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/119434 Closes:
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/119433  Closes:
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/119424 Closes:
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/119423 Closes:
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/119422 Closes:
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/119396 Closes:
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/119395 Closes:
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/119394 Closes:
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/119393

Unmute Esql(Async)SecurityIT tests (#119405)

Those were fixed in https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/119283, but it looks like some CI checks were still running after it got merged, failed, and muted it.

Fixes https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/119268
Fixes https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/119269
Fixes https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/119270
Fixes https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/119271

commit_message:
Fix relocation targets in FieldCapabilitiesIT (#121606)

The test failed because we tried to move a shard to a node that already 
has a copy. This change prevents that from happening.

Closes #119280
Closes #120772

issue_body: [This user](https://discuss.elastic.co/t/incremental-snapshots-are-failing-in-trying-to-write-new-repository-data-over-unfinished-write/372408) has a repository implementation that apparently does not correctly implement the `HeadObject` S3 API. Today's repository analyzer would not detect this incompatibility. We should fix that, for instance by having `GetBlobChecksumAction` sometimes (NB not always) call `BlobContainer#blobExists` before reading the blob contents.
commit_message: Adding a check to verify that BlobContainer::blobExists works as expected (#146595)
